desc: Turin blends baroque grandeur, serious museums, and a lived-in café culture against an Alpine backdrop. It is less overtouristed than Rome or Florence, with strongest visitor demand in mild spring/autumn months and ski-linked winter weekends, while summer is generally easier for availability despite warm afternoons.
hls: Mole Antonelliana and National Cinema Museum
Elegant arcaded boulevards and Savoy architecture
Strong chocolate, aperitivo, and Piedmont cuisine culture
Easy access to Langhe wine country and Alpine valleys
Good rail links to Milan, Genoa, and French-border routes

todo.name: Mole Antonelliana panoramic lift + Cinema Museum
todo.desc: Go early for shorter lift queues and skyline views to the Alps on clear days.

todo.name: Museo Egizio deep-dive visit
todo.desc: Plan a half day for one of Europe's strongest Egyptology collections.

todo.name: Royal Palace, Armory, and Piazza Castello arcades
todo.desc: See Savoy ceremonial spaces and Turin's monumental civic core in one walkable cluster.

todo.name: Porta Palazzo market + Quadrilatero food stops
todo.desc: Pair market browsing with casual Piedmont tastings and aperitivo bars.

todo.name: Superga Basilica tram/rail trip
todo.desc: Climb above the city for broad valley and mountain panoramas.

todo.name: Parco del Valentino and Po riverside cycle
todo.desc: Low-stress green corridor for a break between museums and cafés.

todo.name: Juventus Museum & stadium tour
todo.desc: Popular for football fans; prebook on match weekends.

todo.name: Langhe (Alba/Barolo) day trip
todo.desc: Use Turin as a base for vineyard towns and Piedmont food routes.

wifi.note: Generally dependable in central hotels/apartments and university districts; coworking choices are smaller than Milan but workable for short remote stays.

prac.fltNote: Direct Gatwick–Turin flights are typically seasonal, so year-round routing often uses Milan Malpensa/Linate/Bergamo with onward rail. Return fares are often ~£50–£120 in off-peak, ~£120–£200 in shoulder demand, and ~£200+ around ski season Saturdays and school-holiday peaks.
prac.visa: Italy is in Schengen; UK and US passport holders can generally visit visa-free up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
alerts: Ski-season weekends can tighten rail and hotel availability due to Alpine traffic flows.
Autumn fair/event periods (including truffle and food circuits in wider Piedmont) can raise regional accommodation demand.
Winter fog and occasional snow can affect road transfers more than rail services.

sweet: May–June and September–October are the strongest all-round windows; December to February suits city + ski combinations if you book transport early.
